ontology.foodon_gap_note: No exact FoodOn entry for carrot-potato-pork-rib soup; closest match is generic pork soup (FOODON_03315671). Exact multi-ingredient soup entry not available in FoodOn as of 2024.
ontology.foodex2_description: Pork soup — FoodEx2 composite dish code for pork-based soup preparations
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP used as closest defensible match for pork-based clear soup; no specific carrot-potato-pork-rib soup code exists in FoodEx2 as of 2024.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code identified for this specific soup preparation; SNOMED CT does not catalogue composite Asian soup dishes at this level of specificity.

serving.natural_language_portion: One typical serving is approximately 250 ml (250 g) of soup, which includes broth, pieces of pork rib, carrot, and potato.

nutrient_source.title: Carrot, potato and pork ribs soup — HPB Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for Carrot, potato and pork ribs soup (F06100011). All core macros and secondary nutrients are taken from this canonical HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from USDA FoodData Central pork rib soup analogue (USDA SR Legacy #07213) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry; see notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for carrot-potato-pork-rib soup. GI estimated at 54 (low) based on analogous mixed vegetable soup with potato as the primary carbohydrate contributor. Potato soup GI is approximately 54 per University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care). The soup form significantly dilutes the carbohydrate density; effective GI is moderated by protein from pork ribs and fibre from carrot. GL per 250 g serving = (54 × 3.65 × 2.5) / 100 ≈ 4.9, rounded to 5.0.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content at 314 mg per 100 g; a 250 ml serving delivers approximately 785 mg sodium, representing 34% of the 2300 mg daily limit.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit portion size to 150–200 ml per serving, avoid adding salt during cooking, and skim surface fat after simmering to reduce both sodium and saturated fat load.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low carbohydrate density at 3.65 g per 100 g with sugar at 1.96 g per 100 g; the soup matrix and protein from pork ribs moderate postprandial glucose response.
type2_diabetes.guidance: This soup is suitable for individuals with Type 2 Diabetes when served without added rice or noodles; limit potato quantity in the recipe and favour a larger carrot proportion to further reduce the glycaemic impact.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Pork ribs contribute saturated fat (estimated ~0.95 g per 100 g) and dietary cholesterol; sodium at 314 mg per 100 g also poses a cardiovascular risk at larger serving sizes.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should skim visible fat from the soup surface after cooking, limit portion to 200 ml, choose leaner cuts or reduce rib quantity, and avoid adding salt to keep total sodium within safe limits.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Pork ribs are red meat containing arachidonic acid (omega-6 fatty acid) which amplifies pro-inflammatory eicosanoid pathways relevant to rheumatoid arthritis.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should substitute pork ribs with chicken or fish to eliminate the primary pro-inflammatory red meat component; also monitor response to potato as a nightshade vegetable.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Carrot, potato and pork ribs soup is a traditional Singaporean Chinese clear soup prepared by slow-simmering pork spare ribs with sliced carrots and diced potatoes in water for 1–2 hours until the broth is flavourful and the vegetables are tender. The soup has a light, savoury, mildly sweet flavour from the carrots and a clean, collagen-rich broth from the pork ribs. It is a staple home-cooked dish in Singapore Chinese households, commonly served as a side soup alongside steamed rice. Nutritionally, per 100 g it provides 53 kcal, 3.48 g protein, 2.72 g fat, and 3.65 g carbohydrate, with 314 mg sodium and 0.74 g dietary fibre. The low carbohydrate density makes it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, though the sodium content requires portion control for those with hypertension or cardiovascular disease. The pork rib base makes it unsuitable for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis due to arachidonic acid content. It is gluten-free and dairy-free. The dish is not halal due to the pork content.

confidence_reason: Core macros sourced directly from HPB Singapore Foodo canonical database entry F06100011 with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA SR Legacy pork rib soup analogue and documented. GI estimated from University of Sydney GI Database analogous potato soup entry with full estimation basis documented.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (0.95 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for pork rib soup (closest approved-source analogue) as HPB does not separately report saturated fat for this entry. 2. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, trans_fat_g, potassium_mg, calcium_mg, iron_mg, cholesterol_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and fatty acid fractionation data for this specific Singapore Chinese pork rib soup preparation are not available in the HPB Singapore Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. 3. GI is an analogous estimate based on potato soup from the University of Sydney GI Database; no direct GI measurement for this composite soup exists in approved sources. 4. The dietary tag 'keto' from the input record has been removed as the dish contains potato (a starchy carbohydrate) and is not consistent with a ketogenic dietary profile. 5. is_halal is set to false as the dish contains pork.
